title: Wistful Spanish Tofu Bowl
description: A fresh and romantic Spanish-inspired tofu bowl with parsley, wok-hei and a Korean brass bowl presentation. The dish combines shy flavors and bold textures in a single, temple-friendly vessel.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and add diced tofu, sautéing until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Add soaked and drained quinoa to the skillet, stir to combine with tofu, and pour in the wok-hei, cooking for another 3 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce, lemon zest, and roasted red pepper strips, allowing flavors to meld while the mixture simmers, about 2 minutes.
Transfer the tofu-quinoa mixture into a Korean brass bowl, creating a layered base of greens and protein.
Sprinkle chopped parsley, black olives, and seaweed flakes over the top, ensuring a cohesive, shaggy presentation.
Drizzle the remaining wok-hei over the bowl for a fragrant aroma.
Garnish with a final zest of lemon and a few fresh parsley leaves for a romantic touch.
Serve immediately, letting diners collect the bowl by the same Korean brass vessel, preserving the temple-friendly vibe.
